F ern P olrrrcel P RACTTcEs C olarurs sroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 1,2007
Monte R. Hammer
10300 Pump House Rd.
Banning, California 92220
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-07-075
Dear Mr. Hammer:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post-governmental
employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because you have not provided
any facts related to a specific appearance before or communication with your previous
govemment agency employer, we are treatingyour request as one for informal assistance.2 This
letter should not be construed as assistance on any conduct thatmay have already taken place. In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission
("Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Ogtesby (1975)
FPPC Ops.71.)
Please note that our advice is based solely on the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on
the application, if any, of other post-govemment employment laws such as Public Contract Code
Section 10411. We suggest you seek appropriate legal advice to determine if this or any other
provisions outside the Act apply.
QUESTION
May you accept employment with apivate company that provides services to your
former state employer?
I Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear attitle 2,Sections 1g109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed.)
@!


File No. I-07-075
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prohibit former government employees from accepting positions with
any private employer. However, you are subject to the Act's post-governm^ental employment
restrictions as discussed below.
FACTS
You were a Materials & Research Engineering Associate (Specialist) with the California
Department of Transportation ("Caltrans") for 23 years. In this poritiol you operated under the
direction of a Senior Transportation Engineer and had no rup.*irory responsibilities. your
position was one of three in the Independent Assurance Offiie. your duties were to give written
and practical tests to those seeking Independent Assistance qualification in the California Test
Methods. You also maintained the required documentation associated with these tests. your
position was not designated in the caltrans conflict of interest code.
You are now seeking employment with a private company, 52 Engineering, Inc.
("S2 Engineering"), which has a contract with Caltrans to provide on-call testing services to the
construction division of the agency. You had no business association with 52 Engineering
before it obtained this service contract. Caltrans is also seeking someone who can train their new
employees. As an employee of 52 Engineering, you would be sampling and testing construction
materials and calibrating testing equipment including scales used in thelesting laboratories and
materials production facilities. You would also be providing Caltrans employies training in
California test methods, specification, plant and roadway inspection, and scaie calibration.
Qualification for testers and laboratories to perform the actual testing will still be required
through the lndependent Assurance department of Caltrans.
ANALYSIS
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two tlpes of post-governmental
employment provisions under the Act,3 colloquially known as the:'i"rol,nirg dJo.,, profriUiliorrr.o
We look to these provisions to answer your question.
o Permanent Ban: The first restriction is the "permanent ban" prohibiting a former state
employee from "switching sides" and participating, for compeirsation, in any specific
proceeding involving the State of Califomia or assisting others in the proceeding if the
proceeding is one in which the former state employee participated while employed by the
state (Sections 8740 1 - 87 402, Regulation 1 g7 4l .l).
3 In addition, Section 87407 prohibtts certain state and local officials from making, participating in
making, or using their official position to influence decisions affecting persons with whom they are negotiating
employment, or has any arrangement concerning employment. (Sect[n 87407;Regulation ltil+l .1
4 A comprehensive discussion of the elements of the two laws is set forth in the attached ,.Leaving your
S-tate Job? PosGEmployment Restrictions May AIfect You." We have enclosed this document for your information.
We do not repeat this information in the body of this letter.


File No. I-07-075
Page No. 3
You have not provided any information as to your participation in any proceeding while
employed at Caltrans thatmay affect your ability to engage in any of the conduct you haie
described to us. The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and appliesio any judicial, quasi-judicial.
or other proceeding in which you participated as a state administrative official.t' .iJrdilid,
quasi-judicial or other proceeding' typically involves any proceeding, application, request for a
ruling or other determination, contract, claim, controversy, investigaiionjcharge, accusation,
arrest or other patticular matter involving a specific party or parties in any court or state
administrative agency... ." (Section 87400(c).) Additionally, an official is considered to have
"participated" in a proceeding if he or she took part in the proceeding "personally and
substantially through decision, approval, disapproval, formal written recommendation, rendering
advice on a substantial basis, investigation, or use of confidential information . . ..,, (Section
87400(d).)
"The permanent ban does not apply to a 'new' proceeding even in cases where the
new proceeding is related to or grows out of a prior proceeding in which the
official had participated. A 'new' proceeding not subject to the permanent ban
typically involves different parties, a different subject matter, or diff".errt factual
issues from those considered in previous proceedings." (ftzsf Advice Letter,
No. 4-04-187; see arso Donovan Advice Letter, No. I-03-119.)
To apply the permanent ban to your situation, you need to determine if any of the actions
in which you now wish to engage on behalf of your new employer involve a procleding in which
you participated while employed at Caltrans. (Regutati on 18741.1(a)(+).) If you did not
participate in "a proceeding" as described above, then you are not a state administrative official,
and the permanent ban does not apply to you.
o One-Year Ban: The second restriction is the "one-year ban" prohibiting a state
employee from communicating, for compensation, with his ori", former agency for the
pu{pose of influencing certain administrative or legislative action or influen-cinjcertain
proceedings. (Section 87 406, Regulation I g7 46.1.)
The one-year ban applies to employees who are designated in their former agency,s
conflict of interest code. However, the one-year ban also uppti"s to employees who made or
patticrpated in the making of governmental decisions that had a r"uroruily foreseeable material
effect on any financial interest. In other words, the one-year ban applies tL former employees
who should have been designated in their former employer's code. lSection g7406(d)tl);
Regulation 1 87 a6 J @)(2).)
A state employee "makes a govemmental decision" when acting within the scope of his
or her authority and he or she: 1) votes on a matter; 2) appoints a persoi; 3) obligates his or her
agency to any course of action; 4) enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his or her
agency; or 5) determines not to act, unless the determination is made due to a conflict of interest.
5 Section 87400(b) defines state adminiskative official as "every member, officer, employee or consultant
of a state administrative agency who as part of his or her official responsibilities engages i, urrjriral"iul, quasi-
judicial or other proceeding in other than a purely clerical, secretarial or ministerial-cipacity.,,"


File No. I-07-075
Page No. 4
(Regulation 18702.1.)
Additionally, a state employee "participates in making a govemmental decision,, when he
or she negotiates (without significant.substantive review) with agorr"-*rrrt entity or private
person regarding a governmental decision, or when he or she advises or makes recommendations
to the decisionmaker (either directly or without significant substantive review), by conducting
research or an investigation or by presenting any report, analysis or opinion, which requires tile
exercise of independent judgment on the gart of the employee and the outcome or purpose of
which is to influence the decision. (Regulation 15702.,.)
We have advised that an employee participates in the making of a governmental decision,
even if it is reviewed by several of his or her superiors, if any of the foloriing apply: l) the
superiors rely on the data or analysis prepared by the employee without .t "ctii"g-ii
independently;2) the superiors rely on the professional judgment of the employee; or 3) the
employee in some way actually influences the final decisi oi. ltttyqwsr Advice Letter, No.
M-96-318 fMemorandum to the Attorney General].)
The one-yeatban applies to appearances or communications, for which you are
compensated, made on behalf of any person as an agent, attomey,or representaiive. (Section
87406(d)(L); Regulations 18746.1(bX3) and (a).) From the facts provided you will be working
for 52 Engineering. Since you would be a paid employee yo,, *orld be working for
compensation and in representation of anotherperson.
Appearances and communications are prohibited only if they are made for the ..purpose
of influencing" an "administrative or legislative action" or "discreti onary act involvingifr"
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, gr*i o. contract, or the sale
or purchase of goods or property." (Section 87406(d)(1); Regulation lg746.l(b)(5).) Regulation
18746.2 provides that an appearance or communication is foithe pu{pose of infiuencing if it is
mlde for the principal pu{pose of supporting, promoting, influencing, modifuing, opposing,
delaflng, or advancing the action or proceeding.
FinalTy, appearartces and communications are prohibited if they are before a state agency
the former state employee worked for or represented, tr before a stateigency whose budgJt, 
J
personnel, and othei operations are subject to the control of a state age;ythe former state
employee worked for or represented. (Section S7406(d)(1); Regulatiorif;+e :6)(6).) Since
you left a position with Caltrans, the one-yearbanapplies to any appearance or communications
before caltrans, including all caltrans districts and employees.
You stated in your facts that your former position with Caltrans was not designated in the
Caltrans conflict of interest code, and although there is nothing in your facts or youriuty
statement that indicates your position should have been designated, if you in fact engaged in any
of the above described activities as part of your employmeniwith Caltrans, the one-year ban will


File No. l-07-075
Page No. 5
apply to you from the date you left state employrnent. (Section 87406(dX1); Regulatio n lg746.l
(b)QD' Because you have not provided any facts that indicate that you were making or
participating in the making of governmental decisions in other than a ministerial manner while
employed at Caltrans, we do not further analyzewhether the one-ye arbanapplies to you. If you
believe that you may have engaged in the t1,pe of activities to which ,n. orr"-y"a. ban applies as
described above, please feel free to seek further assistance and include the facts relating to those
activities.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
"il,t/d*H,bru*
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
SKBjgl
Enclosures
7 we have advised that the one-year ban commences when the employee is no longer under an employment
agreement and is no longer receiving compensation, including compensation for "unused,, iacation time. (Weil
Advice Letter, No. A-97-247.)
